claims.claim_text: The default build mode is debug (no -d: flag needed). The compiler reports opt: none (DEBUG BUILD).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Default build output contains 'DEBUG BUILD'.

claims.claim_text: -d:debug is identical to the default build mode (same opt level, same stack trace behavior).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. -d:debug output also contains 'DEBUG BUILD'.

claims.claim_text: Default and -d:debug builds produce full stack traces with file paths and line numbers on unhandled exceptions.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Default build shows full trace with inner and outer frames.

claims.claim_text: -d:release builds show only the raising frame in exception stack traces (no full call chain).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Release shows 'inner' but not 'outer'.

claims.claim_text: -d:danger builds show only the raising frame in exception stack traces (no full call chain).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Danger shows 'inner' but not 'outer'.

claims.claim_text: writeStackTrace() returns 'No stack traceback available' in -d:release mode.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Output contains 'No stack traceback available'.

claims.claim_text: writeStackTrace() returns 'No stack traceback available' in -d:danger mode.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Output contains 'No stack traceback available'.

claims.claim_text: Adding --stackTrace:on --lineTrace:on to -d:release or -d:danger restores full stack traces with file paths and line numbers.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Both 'inner' and 'outer' appear in the restored trace.

claims.claim_text: --lineTrace:on implies --stackTrace:on.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. --lineTrace:on alone restores traces in release mode.

claims.claim_text: --excessiveStackTrace:on (the default) shows full file paths in stack traces. --excessiveStackTrace:off shows only filenames.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. :on has '/path/to/file.nim(' format; :off has 'file.nim(' without leading slash.

claims.claim_text: repr() works on any type and produces a textual representation: ref objects show fields, seqs show @[...], strings show quoted content, raw pointers show hex addresses.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. All repr formats verified: ref='MyRef(x: 42, s: "hello")', seq='@[1, 2, 3]', string='"world"', pointer=hex address.

claims.claim_text: debugEcho compiles inside {.noSideEffect.} procs where echo would be rejected.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. debugEcho in noSideEffect proc compiles and runs.

claims.claim_text: echo inside a {.noSideEffect.} proc causes a compile error.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Compiler error: 'add' can have side effects.

claims.claim_text: --expandMacro:<macro_name> prints the expanded AST as a compiler hint with the tag [ExpandMacro].
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Output contains [ExpandMacro] hint.

claims.claim_text: --expandArc:<proc_name> shows injected =copy, =destroy, =sink, and move calls after ARC/ORC ownership analysis.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. expandArc:extractCopy shows '=copy' in output.

claims.claim_text: --expandArc output is the same under --mm:orc, --mm:arc, and --mm:atomicArc for simple (non-cyclic) ownership patterns.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. All three modes show =copy for extractCopy.

claims.claim_text: --mm:orc is the default memory management mode (cycle-collecting reference counting).
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Default build output contains 'mm: orc'.

claims.claim_text: --debugger:native is equivalent to --debuginfo --linedir:on.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Both compile successfully.

claims.claim_text: AddressSanitizer with --passC and --passL '-fsanitize=address -fno-omit-frame-pointer', -g, -d:noSignalHandler, -d:useMalloc detects heap-buffer-overflow and reports Nim source file/line.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. ASan reports 'AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow' with .nim file reference.

claims.claim_text: -d:useMalloc makes Nim use C's malloc instead of its own allocator. It only works with --mm:arc or --mm:orc.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Compiles and runs with both --mm:orc and --mm:arc.

claims.claim_text: AddressSanitizer requires both --passC and --passL flags; omitting --passL prevents the sanitizer runtime from being linked.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. With only --passC (no --passL), compile fails on this system. The linker needs the sanitizer runtime.

claims.claim_text: Valgrind --leak-check=full --error-exitcode=1 detects memory leaks in Nim binaries compiled without special flags. A clean Nim program reports 0 errors.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. Valgrind reports 'ERROR SUMMARY: 0 errors' for clean program.

claims.claim_text: -d:noSignalHandler prevents Nim's signal handler from intercepting SIGSEGV, allowing ASan to report directly.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. ASan report appears with no mention of Nim signal handler.

claims.claim_text: ASan can be used with clang via --cc:clang in addition to gcc.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. --cc:clang compiles and detects heap-buffer-overflow, but without llvm-symbolizer installed the ASan report shows raw hex addresses instead of Nim source file/line. gcc resolves symbols natively.

claims.claim_text: Windows MSVC ASan uses --cc:vcc --passC:'/fsanitize=address'.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTABLE. No MSVC toolchain available.

claims.claim_text: Using move() instead of direct assignment in a proc causes --expandArc to show 'result = move(...)' instead of '=copy(result, ...)', eliminating a string/seq copy.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. expandArc:extractMove shows 'result = move(obj.data)' with no =copy.

claims.claim_text: --expandArc shows =destroy calls in finally blocks, revealing cleanup order.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. expandArc:main shows =destroy in finally block.

claims.claim_text: ASan reports include Nim source file and line number when compiled with -g or --debugger:native.
claims.evaluation_notes: NUANCED. True for gcc. With clang, llvm-symbolizer must be in PATH; without it, ASan shows raw hex addresses even with -g.

claims.claim_text: echo flushes automatically. stdout.write does not. Short output written with stdout.write may be lost if the program crashes before the buffer drains. Call stdout.flushFile() after stdout.write to guarantee output.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. echo and stdout.write + flushFile both produce output.

claims.claim_text: compiles(expr) returns true if the expression type-checks and compiles, false otherwise. It works at compile time and at runtime as a const bool.
claims.evaluation_notes: DETERMINISTIC. compiles(1 + 2) == true, compiles(1 + "string") == false, compiles works for generic instantiations and type conversions.

corrections.original_claim: ASan reports include Nim source file and line number when compiled with -g or --debugger:native.
corrections.correction: This is true for gcc. With clang, llvm-symbolizer must be installed and in $PATH for the ASan runtime to resolve symbols. Without it, clang ASan shows raw hex addresses even with -g.
